Code Specialty Cases Win % Med QPA Med Award Med Ratio P90 Ratio Max Award Description
Peripheral Nerve dominates volume (89%)
36,699 of 41,011 specialty rows are peripheral nerve — mostly EMG/NCS and nerve blocks. Plastic surgery (1,873) and local tissue rearrangement (2,028) are smaller but have dramatically higher absolute award values.
Rhinoplasty & blepharoplasty: zero federal IDR cases
30400–30420 and 15820–15823 appear nowhere in the dataset. These cases settle during open negotiation, go through state IDR systems, or aren't structured as eligible surprise billing encounters.
Cases by specialty
Median award by specialty
Win rate by specialty
Median ratio (award/QPA) by specialty
Capsulectomy (19371) & fat graft face (15772): ~47× ratio
Best risk-adjusted return in plastic surgery. Both win 93–96% with median awards of $28,000–$40,500. 89% of breast reduction cases come from the NYC metro.
Breast recon flap (19364) wins 97.1% of the time
Highest win rate in all of plastic surgery. Median award $60,000, max $230,000. Tissue expander replacement (11970) has the highest median award at $68,700 with 84.6% win rate.
Plastic surgery CPT codes
CodeCasesWin rateMed QPAMed AwardMed RatioMaxDescription
EMG codes: 38–123× median ratio
EMG/NCS codes dominate volume but have lower absolute awards ($2K–$7K). The ratio is driven by extremely low QPAs ($44–$111). High-value targets: 64483 (epidural injection, $10,327 median award, 90% win rate).
Nerve block codes: high volume, moderate awards
Brachial plexus (64415), femoral (64447), sciatic (64445) are the workhorses. Epidural injections (64483/64484) have the best absolute award-to-volume ratio in peripheral nerve.
Peripheral nerve CPT codes
CodeCasesWin rateMed QPAMed AwardMed RatioMaxDescription
Hand surgery: smaller dataset, solid win rates
264 cases across 16 codes. ORIF distal radius (25605) is highest volume at 50 cases, 90% win rate, $6,364 median award. Flexor tendon repair (26418) shows $14,875 median award with 83% win rate.
Percutaneous fixation (25609) wins 95.8%
Best win rate in hand surgery. $13,000 median award. Open reduction/fixation across hand and wrist codes generally outperforms closed reduction in both win rate and award.
Hand surgery CPT codes
CodeCasesWin rateMed QPAMed AwardMed RatioMaxDescription
14301/14302 (adjacent tissue transfer add-ons): highest volume
271 and 256 cases respectively. Median awards of $15,962 and $13,446. 86–87% win rates. Often billed alongside primary closure codes — excellent batching candidates.
Abdominal wall: laparoscopic hernia repair dominates
136 cases of laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair (49650) with 89.7% win rate and $22,000 median award. Only 9 codes from abdominal wall definition appear in the IDR data.

Abdominal wall reconstruction codes
CodeCasesWin rateMed AwardMed RatioMaxDescription
2–5× QPA: sweet spot for win rate (90–91%)
Across all specialty codes, peak win rates occur at 2–5× QPA. But plastic surgery codes inherently land at 20–50× QPA and still win 80–85% — and when they win, awards are orders of magnitude larger.
Insurers offer exactly 1.00× QPA — always
Median insurer offer is 1.00× QPA. The 75th percentile is 1.38×. Open negotiation is a formality. Plan for IDR from the moment you receive the initial EOB.
